title: Fontaneda's Attested Phrase
context: Demonstrating the only fully attested Calusa sentence from Fontaneda (1575).

lines.speaker: calusa
lines.english: Run to the lookout, see if there are people coming!
lines.notes: This is the only complete Calusa phrase recorded by Fontaneda in 1575. Tier: primary_gloss.

words.english: Run to the lookout, see if there are people coming!

title: Ponce de León's First Contact (1513)
context: Spanish ships anchor off the southwest Florida coast. Calusa warriors paddle out in canoes. The first recorded encounter between Europeans and the Calusa.

lines.speaker: spanish
lines.spanish: Venimos en nombre del Rey de España, nuestro señor. Traemos paz.
lines.english: We come in the name of the King of Spain, our lord. We bring peace.
lines.pronunciation: Venimos en nombre del Rey de España, nuestro señor. Traemos paz.

title: Fontaneda's Captivity (c. 1551)
context: A young Spanish boy, Hernando de Escalante Fontaneda, is shipwrecked on the Florida coast and brought before the Calusa king Carlos. He will live among them for 17 years and become the primary source for the Calusa language.

lines.speaker: spanish
lines.spanish: ¡No me matéis, por amor de Dios! Soy cristiano, no soy sino un niño.
lines.english: Do not kill me, for the love of God! I am a Christian, I am but a child.
lines.pronunciation: ¡No me matéis, por amor de Dios! Soy cristiano, no soy sino un niño.

words.calusa: ¡No me matéis, por amor de Dios! Soy cristiano, no soy sino un niño.

title: Menéndez Meets King Carlos (1566)
context: Pedro Menéndez de Avilés, adelantado of La Florida, arrives at the Calusa capital on Mound Key to negotiate with King Carlos. Menéndez will marry Carlos's sister Doña Antonia to seal an alliance. Based on accounts by Solís de Merás and Barcia.

lines.speaker: spanish
lines.spanish: El Rey Don Felipe, nuestro señor, os ofrece su amistad e protección.
lines.english: King Philip, our lord, offers you his friendship and protection.

title: The Mission at Estero Bay (c. 1697)
context: A Franciscan friar attempts to convert the Calusa at their settlement near Estero Bay during the ill-fated 1697 mission. The Calusa resist, defending their traditional religion centered on the sipi idol. Based on documents translated by Hann (1991).

lines.speaker: spanish
lines.spanish: No hay sino un solo Dios verdadero, el Dios de los cristianos.
lines.english: There is but one true God, the God of the Christians.
lines.pronunciation: No hay sino un solo Dios verdadero, el Dios de los cristianos.
